Start with beginner-friendly courses like Create Your First 2D Game in Unity and Create Your First 3D Game in Unity, then continue into larger projects like Complete Action RPG, Farming RPG, Tower Defense, and Construct a First Person Shooter. Along the way, you’ll learn C# programming, game systems, debugging, optimization, multiplayer, VR, and more. Pay what you want for a complete Unity learning bundle that can help turn your passion for games into real development skills and help support Alzheimer's Research UK with your purchase.

Keys expire. Please redeem your keys before July 30th, 2027.
basic_data.short_marketing_blurb: Learn to make games with Unity
basic_data.description:
Learn to make games with Unity. Take your first steps into game development with one of the world’s most popular game engines, Unity. Whether you’re completely new to coding and game creation or ready to build bigger and more advanced projects, this bundle gives you the tools to grow your skills through hands-on learning. Start with beginner-friendly courses like Create Your First 2D Game in Unity and Create Your First 3D Game in Unity, then continue into larger projects like Complete Action RPG, Farming RPG, Tower Defense, and Construct a First Person Shooter. Along the way, you’ll learn C# programming, game systems, debugging, optimization, multiplayer, VR, and more. Pay what you want for a complete Unity learning bundle that can help turn your passion for games into real development skills and help support Alzheimer's Research UK with your purchase.

Pay $5 or more. Normally, the total cost for the software in this bundle is as much as $1,750. Here at Humble Bundle, you choose the price and increase your contribution to upgrade your bundle! This bundle has a minimum $5 purchase. Pay $5 or more to access keys.

Redeem your keys. All of the software in this bundle is available for Windows, Mac, Android, and Linux.

Support charity. Choose where the money goes - between the publisher and to support Alzheimer's Research UK. If you like what we do, you can leave us a Humble Tip too!

debuggingfoundationsforunity.description_text:
Master debugging principles for Unity games!

Nobody codes perfectly every time, so knowing how to debug projects is an important skill. You'll explore common mistakes and methodologies you can use to debug Unity projects through a series of challenges. These debugging skills will give you the knowledge you need to quickly fix issues with your Unity games and get them to the publishing stage.

You will learn:

  • Common syntax issues
  • How to spot logic problems
  • Solving structure and function calls
  • Error and no error workflows
  • User input nuances
  • Editor-related solutions

... and more!

alzheimersresearchuk.description_text:
If nothing changes, one in two people will be directly affected by dementia. They will either care for someone with the condition, develop it themselves, or both. Today, there are no dementia survivors but research can change this.

Alzheimer’s Research UK is the UK's leading dementia research charity, dedicated to causes, diagnosis, prevention, treatment and cure. Backed by our passionate scientists and supporters, we’re challenging the way people think about dementia, uniting the big thinkers in the field and funding the innovative science that will deliver a cure.

uvmappinginblenderforbeginners.human_name: UV Mapping in Blender for Beginners
uvmappinginblenderforbeginners.description_text:
Master texturing in Blender by learning how to use UV maps!

UV mapping - a process by which you "unwrap" a 3D model into a flat surface - offers creators an easy way to paint complex textures and apply them to 3D models. You'll explore creating and applying UV maps to primitive objects, as well as complex.

Regardless of the sort of model you're making, skills in UV mapping will provide you with key skills to customize your 3D graphics to your exact specifications and allow you to go beyond flat colors for your textures.

You will learn how to:
  • Apply textures to an object
  • Create UV maps for primitive shapes
  • Build models suitable for UV mapping
  • Combine techniques to unwrap complex objects
  • Export UV maps for 2D graphic's software
  • Paint textures onto a flattened UV map

intermediatedesignpatternsincwithunity6.human_name: Intermediate Design Patterns in C# with Unity 6
intermediatedesignpatternsincwithunity6.description_text:
Unlock the power of coding design patterns to enhance your Unity games!

Using coding design patterns can simplify game development projects by making your code more maintainable and reusable. In this course, you'll dive deep into essential coding patterns, starting with the SOLID principles and learning four pivotal coding patterns found across many games. After mastering these patterns, you'll be equipped to tackle complex Unity games with efficient solutions from start to finish no matter the size of the project!

You'll learn how to:

  • Implement SOLID design principles
  • Utilize the Singleton pattern
  • Apply the Observer pattern
  • Execute the Command pattern
  • Integrate Object Pooling
  • Develop flexible, maintainable code

... and more!

resourcegatheringandcraftinginsurvivalun.human_name: Resource Gathering and Crafting in Survival Unity Games
resourcegatheringandcraftinginsurvivalun.description_text:
Master crafting systems for Unity survival games!

Crafting is a core mechanic of survival games - and useful in other genres as well. Using a Unity project with player, items, and inventory ready, you'll learn how to add additional resources and crafting recipes to take your survival game to the next level. These skills and systems cannot only be expanded to suit your needs, but used throughout many future Unity projects.

You will learn how to:

  • Implement equipable items
  • Create first-person item animations
  • Make trees and rocks into resources
  • Set up a crafting UI
  • Use Scriptable Objects for crafting recipes
  • Trigger crafting with a crafting table

... and more!

createatowerdefensegameinunity.human_name: Create a Tower Defense Game in Unity
createatowerdefensegameinunity.description_text:
Dive into strategy game development by one of its most popular subgenres: tower defense games.

Using the powerful Unity engine, you'll build a tower defense game from scratch while exploring a variety of core features needed for the genre, including tower placement, enemy wave spawning, and more. You'll gain skills in creating expandable and adaptable systems for any sort of tower defense project, along with fundamental programming skills in Unity (such as Scriptable Objects) that can easily be translated into other strategy games.

You will learn how to:

  • Create paths for enemies to follow
  • Spawn enemies in customizable waves
  • Place towers with UI indicators
  • Track health and money for gameplay control
  • Implement different towers and attack types
  • Set up win and defeat game over conditions

... and more!

2dproceduralmapsinunityforbeginners.human_name: 2D Procedural Maps in Unity for Beginners
2dproceduralmapsinunityforbeginners.description_text:
Automate the process of building 2D, tile-based game maps with code! Exploring Unity and the powerful C# language, you'll master expandable programming logic to procedurally generate 2D terrain tiles based on various noise maps. Not only will you vastly expand your skills with C#, but also learn fundamentals ways to add variety and replayability to your own game maps, roguelike projects, and more.

You will learn how to:

  • Generate noise maps in Unity
  • Understand noise settings for different effects
  • Calculate biomes based on noise map data
  • Organize biome data with Scriptable Objects
  • Instantiate 2D tiles based on biome data
  • Implement a player controller

createyourfirst2dgameinunity.human_name: Create Your First 2D Game in Unity
createyourfirst2dgameinunity.description_text:
Build your first fully-fledged 2D Unity game step by step!

2D platformers continue to shape the world of indie and classic games, making them a must-learn genre for every aspiring developer. In this course, you’ll construct a 2D platformer from start to finish using Unity, covering essential topics like input controls, enemy AI, collectibles, and level management. By mastering these concepts, you’ll be ready to customize, expand, and design your own platforming experiences - as well as apply key techniques to future 2D game projects.

You’ll learn how to:
  • Set up and slice sprite sheets
  • Implement player movement and input
  • Work with 2D physics and collisions
  • Add animations, sounds, UIs, and more
  • Design multi-level worlds with tilemaps
  • Manage scoring systems

... and more!

complete2dactionrpgunitycourse.human_name: The Complete 2D Action RPG Unity Course
complete2dactionrpgunitycourse.description_text:
Create your own 2D action RPG in Unity!

Action combat systems are considered a staple of the video game industry, and have become ever more popular for 2D RPGs. This comprehensive course will show you how to construct a top-down, action RPG complete with different styles of combat, UI elements, loot drops, tilemaps, and inventory. These action mechanics will vastly expand your skills and allow you to easily integrate this form of gameplay into your systems!

You will learn how to:

  • Move characters and enemies in 2D
  • Implement melee and ranged weapons
  • Set up different, functional items
  • Track player items with an inventory
  • Manage cameras, UIs, and more
  • Handle audio and audio managers

... and more!

bullethellinunity_unit1_basegame.human_name: Bullet Hell in Unity - Unit 1 - Base Game
bullethellinunity_unit1_basegame.description_text:
Master the action-packed mechanics of 2D bullet hells in Unity!

Bullet hell games demand precise controls and complex projectile patterns, teaching you to balance intense action with responsive design. In this course, you will build the foundations of a bullet hell game in Unity - creating custom inputs, modular character components, and core combat logic. This foundation prepares you to expand your bullet hell project with professional-level polish required for commercial-quality development.

You will learn how to:

  • Build custom player input systems
  • Program dynamic projectile movement logic
  • Animate characters using sprite sheets
  • Create reusable health management components
  • Manage precise entity collision detection
  • Architect modular enemy behavior systems

... and more!

bullethellinunity_unit2_gamepolish.human_name: Bullet Hell in Unity - Unit 2 - Game Polish
bullethellinunity_unit2_gamepolish.description_text:
Elevate your bullet hell games to professional levels in Unity!

Polish and performance are the defining traits that separate action game prototypes from commercial-grade titles. Through this course, you will enhance the foundations of a bullet hell project in Unity by implementing an efficient object pooling system, improved game feel elements, and more. These advanced optimization techniques will empower you to scale your game projects and handle complex mechanics in even the most demanding bullet hell titles.

You’ll learn how to:

  • Improve performance with object pooling
  • Construct levels with Unity Tilemaps
  • Manage audio with centralized systems
  • Develop game over game loops
  • Create dynamic damage flash effects
  • Build responsive UI elements

... and more!

createamicroturn-basedrpginunity.human_name: Create a Micro Turn-Based RPG in Unity
createamicroturn-basedrpginunity.description_text:
Explore turn-based combat systems for 2D RPGs!

Turn-based combat remains a popular staple around the world for many 2D RPGs. Through this course, you'll build a micro project in Unity focused specifically on how to build a turn-based combat system with different styles of attack and the Unity UI system. No matter how complex a turn-based system you want to make, this course will make sure you have the fundamentals to create and expand your system however you need for your 2D games!

You will learn how to:

  • Set up 2D turn-based-ready scenes
  • Manage player and enemy turns
  • Build dynamic UIs for combat
  • Implement attack and healing actions
  • Construct enemy AI
  • Render movement and projectiles

introtogamedesign.description_text:
Discover how the core concepts of game design can help you to create fun and intuitive games!

Game design is a fundamental process needed when creating any game - from the smallest platformer to the largest MMORPG. In this course, you'll start mastering the foundations of game design by examining each core component and how they apply to real-world games. By the end, you'll understand both why game design is important and, more crucially, how to apply the concepts to your own games and create a stunning portfolio.

You will learn how to:
  • Plan a game around a core mechanic or experience
  • Engage players by providing tangible goals
  • Structure game actions
  • Guide players through the game
  • Understand game feel and its effects
  • Show players how to play in different ways

... and more!

introtoecsforunity6.description_text:
Revolutionize your Unity projects with ECS!

The Entity Component System (ECS) transforms game development in Unity by providing modern techniques to enhance performance and modularity. In this course, you'll explore the setup, usage, and benefits of ECS within Unity, enabling you to make complex projects with streamlined, efficient code. These skills will empower you to create games and apps that integrate high numbers of objects and calculations for large-scale projects.

You'll learn how to:

  • Set up ECS in Unity
  • Create a massive ball spawner
  • Develop a basic player controller
  • Utilize multi-threading for performance
  • Implement modular programming concepts
  • Understand ECS structure

... and more!

introtovrwithunitysxrinteractiontoolkit.human_name: Intro to VR with Unity's XR Interaction Toolkit
introtovrwithunitysxrinteractiontoolkit.description_text:
Build interactive VR worlds with Unity's XR Interaction Toolkit!

Virtual Reality is revolutionizing how we experience games and applications, making immersive, interactive environments a sought-after skill in modern development. In this course, you'll create a fully-fledged VR kitchen scene - from configuring your XR rig to crafting rich player interactions - using Unity's drag-and-drop XR components. These foundational techniques will help you master the real-world skills needed to bring any of your own VR ideas to life on any compatible headset.

You’ll learn how to:

  • Set up a universal XR rig
  • Implement smooth locomotion and rotation
  • Enable object grabbing and manipulation
  • Create interactive doors and drawers
  • Integrate VR-friendly UI controls
  • Add a custom scripted interaction

... and more!

developapuzzleplatformergameinunity.human_name: Develop a Puzzle Platformer Game in Unity
developapuzzleplatformergameinunity.description_text:
Create a simple platformer in Unity with obstacles, keys, locked doors, and more. Through this course, you will learn several advanced mechanics to create a more puzzle-oriented platformer that challenges your player to explore and unlock color-coded doors. Start your new project today, and make your own tomb diving adventure.

You will learn how to:

  • Setup player controllers (walking, jumping, collecting items, etc.)
  • Utilize Tilemaps and Tilemap collisions to create interesting levels
  • Implement hazards, ladders, keys, and more to encourage player exploration
  • Develop a system to unlock doors based on colored keys
  • Manage saving, loading, and scene transitions for a connected gameplay experience
  • Make a UI system to record a player's number of keys and more

constructamobilephysicsgameinunity.human_name: Construct a Mobile Physics Game in Unity
constructamobilephysicsgameinunity.description_text:
Embrace the unique aspects of mobile touch gestures to build a physics-based Unity game. You'll explore various uses of gravity, drag, collisions, and more to create a game where players are slingshotted at enemies via dragging touch gestures. With the help of the Unity Device Simulator, you'll also learn how to tailor your project to various mobile devices - all skills which can be applied to other mobile and non-mobile game projects!

You will learn how to:

  • Simulate various mobile devices
  • Manipulate Unity's physics
  • Code drag gestures to launch players
  • Improve the camera with parallax
  • Set up enemies and levels with collisions
  • Create a mobile-friendly UI

developacity-buildinggamewithunity.human_name: Develop a City-Building Game with Unity
developacity-buildinggamewithunity.description_text:
Master strategy game mechanics for city-building games - all while creating your own from scratch! Step-by-step, you'll develop an isometric, turn-based city-builder where you can place various buildings and calculate the resources that they generate. You'll learn powerful systems to deal with resource management, and master other crucial game techniques that can be applied to many other types of strategy games.

You will learn how to:

  • Implement a camera that can pan and zoom
  • Set up a grid-based building placement system
  • Detect mouse clicks in 3D space
  • Add different buildings that can be selected
  • Calculate resources based on buildings constructed
  • Relay game information with a UI

... and more!

unityassociateprogrammercertificationpra.human_name: Unity Associate Programmer Certification Practice Exam
unityassociateprogrammercertificationpra.description_text:
Practice for the Unity Associate Programmer Certification exam!

Unity Certification can open new doors of opportunity, so making sure you're ready for the exam is vital to getting industry-recognized credentials. You'll take a series of quizzes aimed at testing your knowledge of C#, asset management, and more to pass the Associate Programmer Certification exam. These quizzes will ensure you're not only ready to pass the exam but are able to program a variety of Unity and C# projects!

You will test yourself on:
  • Using Unity Components
  • Debugging techniques
  • UI systems
  • C# fundamentals
  • Managing scenes & assets
  • Practical programming applications

... and more!

unityminiprojectscfundamentals.description_text:
Kickstart your Unity C# journey with playable mini-projects!

Unity powers some of the world’s most popular games, and mastering its fundamentals opens doors to a wide array of creative opportunities in game development. In this course, you’ll build four mini-games - each focused on core topics like user interaction, physics, and collision detection. By completing these real-world projects, you’ll be ready to prototype your own interactive experiences, expand into more advanced Unity scripting, and create gameplay features with confidence.

You’ll learn how to:
  • Design interactive Unity user interfaces
  • Implement responsive click-based mechanics
  • Apply for loops and optimize code
  • Work with 3D textures and materials
  • Harness Unity’s physics for gameplay
  • Detect and handle collisions via code

... and more!

survivalgameplayermechanicsinunityforbeg.human_name: Survival Game Player Mechanics in Unity for Beginners
survivalgameplayermechanicsinunityforbeg.description_text:
Learn to build survival games in Unity - starting with the player and environment!

From underwater realms to medieval lands, survival games have become a popular staple among developers. You'll explore fundamental mechanics such as movement, needs, health, and a day-night cycle as you construct the beginnings of your very own survival game. These skills will form the core of your survival game projects and teach you universally relevant skills for all games.

You will learn how to:

  • Implement player movement
  • Make a first-person camera
  • Create needs (hunger, thirst, and sleep)
  • Manage health and damage
  • Build a 3D terrain
  • Construct a day-night cycle

... and more!

unity3dmicro-project_pointandclickrpg.human_name: Unity 3D Micro-Project - Point and Click RPG
unity3dmicro-project_pointandclickrpg.description_text:
Build a 3D micro-RPG controlled by your mouse.

Using mouse input is not only a popular way to handle movement in RPGs, but also one with accessibility and cross-platform benefits. In this course, you'll explore the foundations of 3D RPG development in Unity by creating a simple micro-project where players can move around and attack enemies by clicking. These baseline mechanics will provide you with the fundamental knowledge you need to expand and build more complex 3D mechanics in the future.

You will learn how to:

  • Code players and enemies efficiently
  • Detect and process click events
  • Use Unity's NavMesh for auto navigation
  • Make State Machines for enemy AI
  • Implement basic combat and RPG UIs
  • Use Cinemachine to follow players

... and more!

constructafirstpersonshooterinunity.human_name: Construct a First Person Shooter in Unity
constructafirstpersonshooterinunity.description_text:
Develop your own FPS game in Unity!

First-person shooters are not only immensely popular, but also one of the oldest genres in all of video game history. Through this course, you'll build your own FPS game in Unity while learning core mechanics for shooting, player movement, and similar. No matter how complex your planned features are, these FPS essentials will help put you on the path to building your dream FPS project!

You will learn how to:

  • Create a moveable player character
  • Master shooting mechanics with object pooling
  • Implement pathfinding enemies with Unity NavMesh
  • Add health and ammo pickups
  • Set up audio and particle effects
  • Develop FPS-appropriate UIs

... and more!

constructaremotecontrolcarforarinunity.human_name: Construct a Remote Control Car for AR in Unity
constructaremotecontrolcarforarinunity.description_text:
Build a virtual remote control car and then bring it to life with the power of augmented reality using your mobile device's camera.  Not only will you learn how to set up the movement and UI controls for a 3D modeled car, but also how to use Unity and the AR Foundation package for cross-platform development to place and drive your car in the physical world.  Further, you will also take the first steps into occlusion - the process of hiding virtual objects with real-world physical objects - to further blur the line between the virtual and real-world space.

You will learn how to:

  • Create and place a 3D modeled car in AR
  • Script a car model for typical car movement
  • Detect AR planes for your car to drive on
  • Implement UI elements to control your AR car
  • Set up basic occlusion

learnversioncontrolinunity6.description_text:
Transform your Unity development with expert version control techniques!

Version control is an essential skill for developers, allowing seamless collaboration and secure backups for projects. In this course, you'll dive into the dual powerhouses of version control, Unity's built-in version control system and GitHub, while learning to manage your projects from start to finish effectively. With these skills, you'll be able to collaborate smoothly on complex Unity projects, ensuring your work is protected at every step.

You'll learn how to:

  • Set up Unity Version Control
  • Navigate the Unity Version Control interface
  • Integrate Unity with GitHub
  • Create and manage repositories
  • Collaborate with multiple users
  • Pull, push, and commit changes

... and more!

introtoriggingmodelsinblender.human_name: Intro to Rigging Models in Blender
introtoriggingmodelsinblender.description_text:
Prepare your 3D models for animation by learning the fundamentals of rigging!

Rigging is a key animation technique that involves creating a skeletal armature to manipulate a 3D mesh and streamline the animation process. In this course, you'll discover everything required for rigging - from setting up the "bones" to weight painting and controlling which parts of the mesh are affected by each bone. By the end, you'll have the foundations you'll need to master rigging any kind of 3D model and expand your skills in creating custom assets.

You will learn how to:
  • Create bones to build an armature
  • Connect your armature and 3D mesh
  • Control affected vertices with weight painting
  • Export rigged models
  • Apply Mixamo animations to your models

createa3dturn-basedrpginunity.human_name: Create a 3D Turn-Based RPG in Unity
createa3dturn-basedrpginunity.description_text:
Master turn-based RPG combat - one of the most fundamental styles for the RPG genre.

Turn-based combat is a mainstay for many RPGs due to its familiarity for players and the ease of implementation for developers. In this course, you'll master this combat style by building your own 3D RPG from scratch that features different kinds of attacks, customizable enemy encounters, and more. These skills will help you learn transferable skills in creating combat systems for many genres of projects.

You will learn how to:

  • Set up player and enemy sets
  • Manage turns and action displays
  • Implement different attacks & skills
  • Create a simple, combat-ready enemy AI
  • Add UI feedback for gameplay
  • Build a map board with encounters

... and more

exploregameoptimizationinunity6.description_text:
Boost your Unity game performance by exploring optimization techniques!

Game optimization is critical for delivering smooth and efficient gaming experiences - especially in larger-scale projects. In this course, you'll learn how to enhance game performance in Unity by leveraging analysis tools and applying advanced optimization techniques. This knowledge will enable you to craft fully-fledged games that run seamlessly no matter their complexity or size.

You'll learn how to:

  • Utilize Unity’s Profiler effectively
  • Analyze scenes with Frame Debugger
  • Implement static and real-time lighting
  • Apply static batching techniques
  • Optimize with Level of Detail (LOD)
  • Master occlusion culling strategies

... and more!

introtothegamedevelopmentindustry.human_name: Intro to the Game Development Industry
introtothegamedevelopmentindustry.description_text:
Learn how the video game industry works!

Video games are a well-established, profitable industry with unique characteristics that support the development process. You'll explore the industry's structure by learning the overall stages of development, how work is divided by role, and more. This information will help you interact with the game industry with solid footing and have a greater understanding of how games are made.

No prior experience with the game industry is needed.

You'll learn all about:
  • Job roles & responsibilities
  • Large vs. small companies
  • The development cycle
  • IP copyrights
  • NDAs

... and more!

learn3dmodelingwithblenderfromscratch.human_name: Learn 3D Modeling with Blender from Scratch
learn3dmodelingwithblenderfromscratch.description_text:
Master the foundations of Blender and 3D modeling!

3D modeling, the process of creating digital 3D objects, is a robust field of study with real-world applications for animation, game development, and more. In this course, you'll explore the free and open-source Blender software and learn the fundamental techniques for manipulating meshes and creating custom assets. These skills will form the basis for expanding your 3D modeling skills and open up opportunities to create unique assets for your projects.

You will learn how to:
  • Understand how 3D objects work
  • Move, rotate, and scale objects
  • Edit vertices, edges, and faces
  • Work with Blender's modifiers
  • Apply materials to meshes
  • Export models for other programs

... and more!

introtogamedevelopmentwithunity.human_name: Intro to Game Development with Unity
introtogamedevelopmentwithunity.description_text:
Get started with Unity and unlock limitless game creation possibilities!

Unity is the powerful, cross-platform engine behind some of the world’s most popular games and interactive experiences. Through this course, you’ll gain a solid foundation in Unity’s core systems, essential tools, and C# scripting - empowering you to bring your creative ideas to life across any genre or platform. By learning best practices and real-world workflows from start to finish, you’ll be ready to tackle your own projects with confidence and clarity.

You'll learn how to:
  • Install and set up Unity
  • Navigate the Unity Editor interface
  • Manage and manipulate GameObjects
  • Work with lighting, physics, and more
  • Write and integrate basic C# scripts
  • Create your first interactive project

... and more!

introtounityservices.description_text:
Discover built-in development tools for Unity projects!

Unity Services are not only easy to use but save developers time with a variety of useful features for making games. You'll explore four different services offered by Unity that allow you to quickly monetize, analyze, and build your projects. This knowledge will offer you new ways to approach developing your games - whether you're working solo, in a team, or as part of a company.

You'll learn all about:

  • Ads for revenue streams
  • Analytics for tracking key metrics
  • Building to the cloud
  • Adding version control

... and more!

unityassociategamedevelopercertification.human_name: Unity Associate Game Developer Certification Practice Exam
unityassociategamedevelopercertification.description_text:
Prepare for the Unity Associate Game Developer Certification exam!

As one of the few industry-recognized credentials, Unity Certification can help boost your opportunities as a developer. You'll test your knowledge through a series of quizzes on Unity tools and the game development process - topics necessary for the Associate Game Developer Certification exam. These quizzes will both make sure you're ready for the exam and showcase your preparedness to build Unity games!

You will test yourself on:
  • Game design & industry
  • Using the Editor
  • Materials and animation
  • C# foundations
  • Physics, lighting, & audio
  • Unity Services

... and more!

unitysurvivalproject_inventorysystem.description_text:
Enhance your survival game projects by mastering items and inventories.

Collecting items is an integral aspect of not just survival games, but many other genres as well. Working from an existing Unity project with player mechanics ready, you'll explore how to add survival-related items and manage them with an inventory. The systems you build will allow you to expand them to suit your story - and even adapt them to other kinds of Unity projects!

You will learn how to:

  • Create items as Scriptable Objects
  • Set up collectible, in-scene objects
  • Craft an inventory UI
  • Add and select inventory items
  • Manage stackable items
  • Interact with inventory items

... and more!

constructamicro-farmingrpginunity.human_name: Construct a Micro-Farming RPG in Unity
constructamicro-farmingrpginunity.description_text:
Master farming simulation mechanics for RPGs!

Since the SNES era, farming has been a popular addition to games, particularly RPGs. In this course, you'll explore crop-growing mechanics related to this sub-genre, as well as general Unity game development principles with singleton programming patterns, pseudo-state machines, and more. No matter the type of RPG you want to build, these farming mechanics will help you round out your Unity skills and allow you to add variety to your current and future game projects.

You will learn how to:

  • Set up 4-direction player movement
  • Create tilemaps with interactable tiles
  • Store crop data as Scriptable Objects
  • Implement crop growing and watering mechanics
  • Control resources such as money
  • Display information with a UI

... and more!

createyourfirst3dgameinunity.human_name: Create Your First 3D Game in Unity
createyourfirst3dgameinunity.description_text:
Build a complete 3D platformer in Unity from start to finish!

As a foundational genre that has delighted players for decades, 3D platformers offer some of the best real-world opportunities for aspiring developers to master core mechanics. Through this course, you'll develop a fully-fledged 3D platformer with all the fundamental systems that make these games so engaging. These skills will not only give you a finished Unity game for your portfolio but also provide a comprehensive framework for designing your own unique 3D levels and gameplay challenges.

You'll learn how to:
  • Process player input
  • Implement physics-based movement
  • Create enemies and collectibles
  • Set up character animations
  • Build multiple game levels
  • Manage scene loading and transitions

... and more!

alzheimersresearchuk.description_text:
If nothing changes, one in two people will be directly affected by dementia. They will either care for someone with the condition, develop it themselves, or both. Today, there are no dementia survivors but research can change this.

Alzheimer’s Research UK is the UK's leading dementia research charity, dedicated to causes, diagnosis, prevention, treatment and cure. Backed by our passionate scientists and supporters, we’re challenging the way people think about dementia, uniting the big thinkers in the field and funding the innovative science that will deliver a cure.

from_bundle.tile_short_name: Learn to Make Games in Unity 2026 Bundle
from_bundle.short_marketing_blurb: Learn to make games with Unity
from_bundle.marketing_blurb: Get Intro to Game Design, Intro to Unity Services, Intro to Game Development with Unity, and more!
from_bundle.detailed_marketing_blurb:
Take your first steps into game development with one of the world’s most popular game engines, Unity. Whether you’re completely new to coding and game creation or ready to build bigger and more advanced projects, this bundle gives you the tools to grow your skills through hands-on learning. Start with beginner-friendly courses like Create Your First 2D Game in Unity and Create Your First 3D Game in Unity, then continue into larger projects like Complete Action RPG, Farming RPG, Tower Defense, and Construct a First Person Shooter. Along the way, you’ll learn C# programming, game systems, debugging, optimization, multiplayer, VR, and more. Pay what you want for a complete Unity learning bundle that can help turn your passion for games into real development skills and help support Alzheimer's Research UK with your purchase.

Keys expire. Please redeem your keys before July 30th, 2027.
from_bundle.author: Zenva Pty Ltd

basic_data.human_name: Humble Comic Bundle: The Blade Runner Collection by Titan Comics
basic_data.detailed_marketing_blurb: Are you on the side of humanity or technology? Lose yourself in the renowned Blade Runner comic series in this bundle from Titan Comics! Follow the continued stories of Rick Deckard and Roy Batty alongside new original characters in thrilling graphic novels and comics like Blade Runner Black Lotus: Las Vegas #1 and Blade Runner 2019: Vol. 1: Los Angeles, and see a new side of this iconic dystopian universe. Uncover what’s new with the Tyrell Corporation, and help support Direct Relief with your purchase!
basic_data.short_marketing_blurb: Discover the next chapter of Blade Runner’s story
basic_data.description:
Discover the next chapter of Blade Runner’s story. Are you on the side of humanity or technology? Lose yourself in the renowned Blade Runner comic series in this bundle from Titan Comics! Follow the continued stories of Rick Deckard and Roy Batty alongside new original characters in thrilling graphic novels and comics like Blade Runner Black Lotus: Las Vegas #1 and Blade Runner 2019: Vol. 1: Los Angeles, and see a new side of this iconic dystopian universe. Uncover what’s new with the Tyrell Corporation, and help support Direct Relief with your purchase!

Pay $5 or more. Normally, the total cost for the comics in this bundle is as much as $207. Here at Humble Bundle, you choose the price and increase your contribution to upgrade your bundle! This bundle has a minimum $5 purchase.

Read them anywhere. The comics in this bundle are available in CBZ and PDF formats, so they work on your computer, e-readers, iPads, cell phones, and a wide array of mobile devices! Instructions and a list of recommended reading programs can be found here.

Support charity. Choose where the money goes - between the publisher and to support Direct Relief. If you like what we do, you can leave us a Humble Tip too!

bladerunner2019volume1artistsedition.description_text:
A thrilling new saga set in the instantly recognizable, neo-noir world of Blade Runner.

The LAPD’s top Blade Runner, Aahna ‘Ash’ Ashina, has been assigned to investigate the sudden disappearance of the wife and daughter of powerful business tycoon, Alexander Selwyn – a close personal friend of Eldon Tyrell. Ash’s search will take her on a dark, violent journey deep into the crime-ridden underbelly of an alternative, rain-soaked future Los Angeles, where she uncovers a horrifying secret and a desperate conspiracy…

Showcasing the breathtaking black and white artwork of Andres Guinaldo, this exclusive artist’s edition presents issues 1-4 of the smash-hit Blade Runner 2019 series in a truly unique way. Includes never-before-seen concept sketches, issue thumbnails, and an exclusive interview with Guinaldo, conducted by Blade Runner 2019 writers, Michael Green and Mike Johnson.

developers.developer-name: Michael Green, Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo

bladerunner2019volume3_homeagainhomeagai.human_name: Blade Runner 2019 Volume 3: Home Again, Home Again
bladerunner2019volume3_homeagainhomeagai.description_text:
Los Angeles, 2026.

Ex Blade Runner Aahna ‘Ash’ Ashina returns to Los Angeles to settle the score with agribusiness tycoon Alexander Selwyn who had sold his daughter, Cleo, to Eldon Tyrell for genetic experimentation.

In a city still suffering from the effects of the Black Out, Ash – once the huntress – is now the hunted, as the LAPD, the underworld, and a rogue group of Replicants search the city looking for her.

With no friends and a price on her head, nowhere is safe for Ash. She is forced to turn for help from an unlikely source as she searches for a lead to Selwyn and a bloody reckoning…

Collects Blade Runner 2029 #9-12.

“An utterly immersive experience… an emotionally engaging story, and exquisite artwork. Don’t miss out on this one.” – Flickering Myth

developers.developer-name: Michael Green, Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

bladerunner2029_vol1.description_text:
From the rain-soaked dystopian future of Los Angeles comes a new saga set in the world of Blade Runner.

LAPD’s best Blade Runner and detective, Aahna ‘Ash’ Ashina, has been assigned to investigate the mysterious disappearance of Isobel and Cleo Selwyn, the wife and daughter of business tycoon Alexander Selwyn – a close personal friend of Eldon Tyrell.

Ash’s search will take her on a journey deep into the crime-ridden underbelly of Los Angeles – a slowly decaying megacity – as she uncovers a terrible secret and a desperate conspiracy that forces her to confront her own hatred for Replicants – the synthetic humans – that she hunts and kills with such vengeance.

Collects Blade Runner 2019 #1-4.

“Blade Runner 2019 hits the ground running in what looks to be a must-read new entry in the franchise.” – AIPT!

“A worthy new Blade Runner tale with a new, very interesting lead character.” – Newsarama

developers.developer-name: Michael Green, Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

bladerunner2029volume3_redemption.description_text:
Early in the 21st Century, the Tyrell Corporation advanced Robot technology evolution into the Nexus phase – a being virtually identical to a human – known as a Replicant.

Replicants were used Off-world as slave labor. Those who escaped to Earth were hunted by Blade Runner units ordered to kill any trespassing Replicant upon detection.

In 2022, a Replicant attack on the Tyrell Corporation forced the company into bankruptcy and erased all records of existing Replicants. The surviving Nexus 8 models disappeared with the help of the Replicant Underground. Many Replicants remained in servitude.

In 2027, Aahna “Ash” Ashina, a former Blade Runner, rejoined the department to hunt down fugitive Replicants. Her superiors are unaware that her loyalties are divided. Her lover, Freysa, is a leader in the Replicant Underground.

While investigating a report of a rogue Replicant, Ash discovered that a Nexus 6 Replicant, Yotun, whom she had failed to retire 12 years previously, had somehow outlived his expiration date and become the leader of a Replicant guerrilla army.

Yotun captured Ash and ordered his army to launch an all-out attack upon Los Angeles, and called on all Replicants in the city to revolt against their masters. Then he destroyed the Seawall and took over the LAPD Tower before rigging it for demolition.

Rescued from Yotun’s clutches by members of the Replicant Underground, Ash raced back to the city to save her lover, whom Yotun had mistakenly believed had joined his cause. Instead, she sabotaged his plan and in retaliation he blinded her in one eye and left her to die in the burning LAPD Tower.

Faced with killing Yotun or rescuing Freysa, Ash chose her wounded lover, leaving Yotun to escape.

Collects #9-12 of Blade Runner 2029.

developers.developer-name: Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

bladerunner2029_vol1_reunion.description_text:
Los Angeles, 2029.

Aahna ‘Ash’ Ashina is back with the LAPD Blade Runner Unit and has returned to the rain-soaked streets of a slowly crumbling Los Angeles, hunting for renegade Replicants.

But this time she’s out to save as many of them as she can. Changed by her experiences, Ash is no longer the same cold, cynical killing machine as she was in 2019. Now she has a new agenda that, if discovered, will make her the enemy of humanity.

When Ash is confronted by an old adversary, one long believed dead, she is drawn into a deadly cat-and-mouse game with a Replicant unlike any she has ever faced before – a Replicant with no fear of death and with a dream to finish off what the Blackout started in 2022…

Collecting issues #1-4 of the critically acclaimed, smash hit series Blade Runner 2029.

“We’re authentically back in the dystopian world of Blade Runner, and I couldn’t be happier. 9 out of 10.” – Sci-Fi Bulletin

“Beautifully done… Demonstrates with potent skill that Blade Runner 2029 will stand definitively in its own right.” – Set the Tape

“A must-read for Blade Runner and Cyberpunk fans everywhere.” – Monkeys Fighting Robots

developers.developer-name: Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

bladerunneroriginsvolume2_scrap.description_text:
Los Angeles, 2009.

Someone is triggering psychotic breakdowns in the NEXUS 4 REPLICANT population, sending once obedient servants on homicidal rampages. Ex-LAPD Detective Cal Moreaux has been ordered to hunt down and kill the instigator.

Tasked by the Tyrell Corp executive Ilora Stahl to find a rogue Nexus 5 prototype, believed to be responsible for the attacks, Cal’s hunt has brought him back to the slum sector of LA where he and his sister grew up.

Now, with time running out, Cal must unlock the shocking secret that led a Tyrell Corp scientist to commit suicide, and stop the renegade Replicant before it can corrupt the entire city.

Written by the critically acclaimed writing team of K. Perkins and Mellow Brown, and illustrated by award-winning artist Fernando Dagnino, and colorist Marco Lesko.

“A series that perfectly captures the spirit of the Blade Runner universe.” – Comic Book Resources

“A riveting mystery that is both familiar and refreshing at the same time.” – Syfy Wire

developers.developer-name: K. Perkins; Mellow Brown; Fernando Dagnino; Marco Lesko

bladerunneroriginsvolume3_burning.description_text:
Los Angeles, 2009.

Tyrell Corp executive Ilora Stahl has instigated a brutal purge of LA’s Sector 6-B, in an attempt to kill a rogue Nexus 5 prototype who is responsible for triggering an uprising of Nexus 4 Replicants.

Now, with the sector ablaze, LAPD detective Cal Moreaux has joined forces with a former Tyrell Corp scientist, and a band of Replicant rebels and human sympathizers to stop the prototype and save the streets he and his sister once called home.

Collects Blade Runner: Origins #9-12.

“A series that perfectly captures the spirit of the Blade Runner universe.” – Comic Book Resources

developers.developer-name: K. Perkins; Mellow Brown; Fernando Dagnino; Marco Lesko

bladerunneroriginsvolume1_product.description_text:
Los Angeles, 2009

When the body of a Tyrell Corporation scientist working on an experimental new type of Replicant is discovered in her laboratory, an apparent suicide, LAPD detective Cal Moreaux is assigned to the case. Determined to find the truth behind the seemingly routine suicide, he encounters a deadly conspiracy within the Tyrell Corporation itself.

Set ten years before the events of the critically acclaimed Blade Runner 2019 comic book series and the original Ridley Scott film, this in-canon prequel sees the birth of the Blade Runner department and introduces a new hero to the Blade Runner pantheon.

Featuring stunning art by Fernando Dagnino and Marco Lesko, and a grippng story by K. Perkins & Mellow Brown, and Mike Johnson.

“It is simply perfect! An absolute must-have for Blade Runner and cyberpunk fans alike.” – Big Comic Page

“From the distinct cityscape to the 1920s look of the police station, it looks perfect. Top marks to the art team.” – The Pullbox

developers.developer-name: K. Perkins; Mellow Brown; Fernando Dagnino; Marco Lesko

bladerunner2039volume3_ash.description_text:
THE ASH SAGA CONCLUDES!

Twenty years ago, Aahna “Ash” Ashina was the LAPD’s most efficient and deadly Blade Runner, hunting Replicants with a zeal unrivalled. Now, two decades later, Ash lives as a fugitive helping Replicants escape their enslaved servitude and survive undetected on Earth.

Convinced that the secret of Replicant reproduction lies hidden in the DNA of Cleo Selwyn, a young woman whom Ash helped to escape back in 2019, Niander Wallace has unleashed his Replicant protegee, Luv – LAPD’s first Replicant Blade Runner, and a clone of Ash in her prime to bring them to him dead or alive.

For twenty years, Ash has been running. Today, she stops…

Collects Blade Runner 2039 #9-12

“Johnson’s plotting, pacing and dialog are excellent, and Guinaldo’s art is outstanding.” – Comical Opinions.

developers.developer-name: Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

bladerunnertokyonexus_toloseistowin_issu.human_name: Blade Runner Tokyo Nexus: To Lose is to Win #1
bladerunnertokyonexus_toloseistowin_issu.description_text:
The appearance of unsanctioned Replicant technology is the Far East has forced the Tyrell Corporation to ruthless protect its monopoly on synthetic humans. Blade Runner Rumiko and a crew of hunters were dispatched on their behalf to aggressively eliminate any threat to their intellectual property. Rumiko travelled to Japan and learned that a shadowy research laboratory called Cheshire, financed by a powerful Tokyo Yakuza family, was behind the counterfeit Replicants.

Meanwhile in Tokyo, ex-Marine Mead and Replicant Combat Model Stix worked as unofficial Private Investigators while searching for the traitor who betrayed them during an off-the-books mission to Kalanthia that had killed the rest of their squad. They had been testing a new neural interface codenamed Aurora, when the unknown traitor stole the device, killed the rest of their squad and escaped back to Earth. The stolen tech enabled a human operator to mentally synch with a Replicant combat model to enhance combat capabilities of both, whilst simultaneously recording the brainwave patterns of the Replicant Combat Model.

A young woman named Sayo hired Mead and Stix to find Miyuko, her missing partner, and the investigation drew them both into a turf war between two warring families of Yakuza. A spree of brutal killings using his unique M.O. made Stix the prime suspect, forcing him to go on the run, and led to an alliance between Mead and Rumiko. It subsequently transpired that a Cheshire clone of Stix had been implanted with his memories and was committing the killings.

Now, while Rumiko searches for Aurora, Stix and Mead find themselves working for the Yakuza and running from Tyrell’s hunters.

developers.developer-name: Nancy A. Collins; Mariano Taibo; Marco Lesko

bladerunner2039_vol1_luv.description_text:
Los Angeles, 2039.

Twenty years ago, former Blade Runner Aahna ‘Ash’ Ashina helped a young girl and her surrogate Replicant mother escape from the clutches of her sadistic father – the business tycoon, Alexander Selwyn. Now, Cleo Selwyn has returned from the Off-world colony of Arcadia to Los Angeles in search of her ‘mother’, Isobel, whom she believes is the victim of an abduction orchestrated by Niander Wallace.

For three years, Niander Wallace has been manufacturing his new line of ‘perfectly obedient’ Replicants and now, in 2039, his personal assistant, Luv, has become the first Replicant to be assigned to the LAPD Blade Runner Division.

Searching for answers, Cleo sets out to track-down Ash, who for the past ten years has been living off the grid, unaware that Luv has been ordered to find and retire anyone who comes to her aid…

Collects Blade Runner 2039 #1-4.

“Blade Runner continues to ride a euphoric high in its storytelling.” – Set The Tape

developers.developer-name: Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

bladerunnerblacklotusvolume1_leavingla.human_name: Blade Runner Black Lotus Volume 1: Leaving L.A.
bladerunnerblacklotusvolume1_leavingla.description_text:
Los Angeles, 2032.

The prototype Replicant known only as Elle – aka The Black Lotus Killer, has escaped from the clutches of her creator Niander Wallace and headed out into the desert in search of answers to her mysterious past and a possible redemption.

But deep in the desert wasteland, Elle finds herself stranded in the industrial settlement of Fracktown and drawn into a deadly dispute between the town’s two warring factions that could bring her face-to-face with the one person she is trying to escape from… Niander Wallace.

Written by award-winning horror writer Nancy A. Collins, and illustrated by fan favorite Enid Balam, Blade Runner: Black Lotus heralds a new chapter in the saga of Blade Runner.

developers.developer-name: Nancy A. Collins; Enid Balam; Marco Lesko

bladerunner2029volume2_echoes.description_text:
Los Angeles, 2029.

The city is in chaos, reeling from a Replicant uprising led by Yotun – a genetically defective Nexus 6 who has somehow managed to outlive his four-year lifespan. With Blade Runner Ash his prisoner, the newly completed seawall defenses smashed, and the ruling elite massacred, Yotun’s guerrilla army of fanatical Replicants have moved in to take control and realize Yotun’s dream of a city of humans ruled by Replicants.

Now it’s up to Ash to find a way of escaping and stopping Yotun before he burns the city to the ground and kills the woman she loves.

Written by New York Times best-selling author Mike Johnson and drawn by fan-favorite artist Andres Guinaldo.

Collects #5-8 of Blade Runner 2029.

“A must-read for Blade Runner and cyberpunk fans everywhere.” – Monkeys Fighting Robots

“We’re authentically back in the dystopian world of Blade Runner, and I couldn’t be happier. 9 out of 10.” – Sci-Fi Bulletin

developers.developer-name: Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

bladerunner2019volume2_offworld.description_text:
In 2019, the Blade Runner known as Aahna ‘Ash’ Ashina rescued Cleo Selwyn, daughter of business tycoon Alexandra Selwyn, from a plot to give the girl to the Tyrell corporation for genetic experimentation.

Ash and Cleo escaped Los Angeles and disappeared.

Now, seven years later and living under assumed identities, Ash and Cleo have resurfaced on one of the Off-world mining colonies. But Cleo’s father has never stopped looking for them, sending out dozens of bounty hunters and search parties to kill Ash and bring his daughter home. Now, following a bloody Replicant mutiny, Ash and Cleo have become separated and a ruthless new Blade Runner called Hythe has Ash dead in her sights.

Collects Blade Runner 2019 #5-8.

“10 out of 10. A must-read title.” – Bleeding Cool

developers.developer-name: Michael Green, Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

directrelief.description_text:
Donations to support Direct Relief are made to the PayPal Giving Fund, subject to its policies.

Founded in 1948, Direct Relief is a nonprofit, nonpartisan organization that has become one the world’s largest providers of medical relief in the world. Direct Relief works with healthcare professionals and organizations on the ground, establishing partnerships with local organizations that provide health services to people who would otherwise not receive them. Active in all 50 states and in 70 countries, Direct Relief is the only U.S. nonprofit organization licensed and accredited to distribute prescription medications in all 50 U.S. states.  Among its distinctions, Direct Relief has top charity ratings, including four-star and "top-notch" ratings from Charity Navigator, a 100% fundraising efficiency rating from Forbes magazine, and was recently recognized by Fast Company as one of the Top 10 Most Innovative Nonprofits in the World.

Direct Relief’s ongoing partnerships allow it to respond fast and expansively in times of emergency, including the devastating earthquakes in Nepal, where it has provided over $28 million (wholesale value) in medical aid to date.  This includes supplies for surgeries and treatment of trauma-related injuries, antibiotics, nutritional items, and other critical medical resources.

bladerunner2039volume2_upgrade.description_text:
After 20 years living Off-world. Cleo Selwyn has returned to Los Angeles in search for her surrogate Replicant mother Isobel, whom Cleo believes has been abducted on the orders of Niander Wallace.

Believing that Cleo’s DNA holds the secret of Replicant fertility, Wallace has ordered his own personal Replicant assistant, Luv, the first Replicant Blade Runner, to hunt her down with approval to kill any who stand in her way.

Now reunited with her one-time protector and former Blade Runner Aahna ‘Ash’ Ashina, Cleo and Ash set off on a desperate race to find her mother and escape back Off-world before Luv can find them.

Collects Blade Runner 2039 #5-9

developers.developer-name: Mike Johnson; Andres Guinaldo, Marco Lekso

bladerunnerblacklotus_lasvegas_issue1.human_name: Blade Runner Black Lotus: Las Vegas #1
bladerunnerblacklotus_lasvegas_issue1.description_text:
LOS ANGELES, 2032

Waking up in a derelict complex somewhere in the desert, a young woman called Elle, with no memory of her past, discovered that she and several other people have been brought there to be hunted for sport in a sadistic big-game safari.

Displaying incredible survival and combat skills, only Elle managed to escape the hunt and she found herself drawn to the nearest city: Los Angeles.

There she learned that she was a prototype Replicant codenamed Black Lotus, created by Niander Wallace Jnr as an unwitting assassin and part of his plot to seize control of the Wallace Corporation from his father.

Following a violent confrontation with Niander Wallace Jnr that left him blind and his father, Wallace Snr, and Elle’s Replicant sister White Lotus dead, Elle fled into the desert on a Spinner bike in search of a new life, free from violence, bloodshed and death.

She subsequently found safe haven with an environmental commune living sustainably in the desert, but was forced to defend it from attack from a nearby fracking plant. The resulting battle destroyed the plant and saved the community but also alerted Niander to the fact that Elle was still alive.

Once again, Elle set off into the desert, unaware that Niander, still obsessed with his young Replicant assassin, was determined to recapture her at any cost…

developers.developer-name: Nancy A. Collins; Jesus Hervas; Marco Lesko

bladerunnertokyonexusvolume1_dieinpeace.human_name: Blade Runner Tokyo Nexus Volume 1: Die In Peace
bladerunnertokyonexusvolume1_dieinpeace.description_text:
Two marines – the human Mead, and Stix, a modified Replicant – had been left for dead after their Off-world mission was betrayed. Having escaped back to Earth and the neon underbelly of Tokyo, they survive as off-the-grid private detectives while searching for the traitor who killed their comrades.

Searching for a missing woman ensnares Mead and Stix into a deadly power struggle between rival Yakuza clans and the secretive Cheshire Group, a ‘yami kigyo’ (“shadow corporation”), with its own brand of death-dealing combat Replicants.

But of greater concern to the bootleg detectives is the elite Blade Runner sent by the Tyrell Corporation to track them down.

In a city where loyalty is a commodity and survival never guaranteed, Mead and Stix must outsmart them all – or die trying.

Collects Blade Runner: Tokyo Nexus #1-4

developers.developer-name: Kianna Shore; Mariano Taibo; Marco Lesko

directrelief.description_text:
Donations to support Direct Relief are made to the PayPal Giving Fund, subject to its policies.

Founded in 1948, Direct Relief is a nonprofit, nonpartisan organization that has become one the world’s largest providers of medical relief in the world. Direct Relief works with healthcare professionals and organizations on the ground, establishing partnerships with local organizations that provide health services to people who would otherwise not receive them. Active in all 50 states and in 70 countries, Direct Relief is the only U.S. nonprofit organization licensed and accredited to distribute prescription medications in all 50 U.S. states.  Among its distinctions, Direct Relief has top charity ratings, including four-star and "top-notch" ratings from Charity Navigator, a 100% fundraising efficiency rating from Forbes magazine, and was recently recognized by Fast Company as one of the Top 10 Most Innovative Nonprofits in the World.

Direct Relief’s ongoing partnerships allow it to respond fast and expansively in times of emergency, including the devastating earthquakes in Nepal, where it has provided over $28 million (wholesale value) in medical aid to date.  This includes supplies for surgeries and treatment of trauma-related injuries, antibiotics, nutritional items, and other critical medical resources.

from_bundle.tile_short_name: The Blade Runner Collection by Titan Comics
from_bundle.short_marketing_blurb: Discover the next chapter of Blade Runner’s story
from_bundle.marketing_blurb: Get Blade Runner 2039 Volume 1: Luv, Blade Runner 2039 Volume 3: Ash, Blade Runner 2019 Volume 2: Off World, and more!
from_bundle.detailed_marketing_blurb: Are you on the side of humanity or technology? Lose yourself in the renowned Blade Runner comic series in this bundle from Titan Comics! Follow the continued stories of Rick Deckard and Roy Batty alongside new original characters in thrilling graphic novels and comics like Blade Runner Black Lotus: Las Vegas #1 and Blade Runner 2019: Vol. 1: Los Angeles, and see a new side of this iconic dystopian universe. Uncover what’s new with the Tyrell Corporation, and help support Direct Relief with your purchase!
from_bundle.author: Titan Publishing Group

basic_data.human_name: Humble Tech Book Bundle: Linux: All the Things by O'Reilly
basic_data.detailed_marketing_blurb: Tired of that stale operating system? Add color, customization, and optimization to your workstation with the all-new Linux: All the Things Bundle by O'Reilly! Master the fundamentals of Linux with 15 eBooks that cover everything you need to know. Pay what you want for an expertly curated Linux library designed to streamline your learning journey with foundational eBooks like Learning Git, Linux Pocket Guide, and Practical Linux System Administration. Unlock what’s possible with Linux, and help support Code for America with your purchase!
basic_data.description:
Learn Linux with O’Reilly. Tired of that stale operating system? Add color, customization, and optimization to your workstation with the all-new Linux: All the Things Bundle by O'Reilly! Master the fundamentals of Linux with 15 eBooks that cover everything you need to know. Pay what you want for an expertly curated Linux library designed to streamline your learning journey with foundational eBooks like Learning Git, Linux Pocket Guide, and Practical Linux System Administration. Unlock what’s possible with Linux, and help support Code for America with your purchase!

Pay $5 or more. Normally, the total cost for the ebooks in this bundle is as much as $870. Here at Humble Bundle, you choose the price and increase your contribution to upgrade your bundle! This bundle has a minimum $5 purchase.

Read them anywhere. The ebooks in this bundle are available in PDF, and ePub formats, so they work on your computer, e-readers, iPads, cell phones, and a wide array of mobile devices! Instructions and a list of recommended reading programs can be found here.

Support charity. Choose where the money goes - between the publisher and to support Code for America. If you like what we do, you can leave us a Humble Tip too!

networkprogrammabilityandautomation.description_text:
Network engineers are finding it harder than ever to rely solely on manual processes to get their jobs done. New protocols, technologies, delivery models, and the need for businesses to become more agile and flexible have made network automation essential. The updated second edition of this practical guide shows network engineers how to use a range of technologies and tools, including Linux, Python, APIs, and Git, to automate systems through code. This edition also includes brand new topics such as network development environments, cloud, programming with Go, and a reference network automation architecture.

Network Programmability and Automation will help you automate tasks involved in configuring, managing, and operating network equipment, topologies, services, and connectivity. Through the course of the book, you'll learn the basic skills and tools you need to make this critical transition.

You'll learn:

  • Programming skills with Python and Go: data types, conditionals, loops, functions, and more
  • New Linux-based networking technologies and cloud native environments, and how to use them to bootstrap development environments for your network projects
  • Data formats and models: JSON, XML, YAML, Protobuf, and YANG
  • Jinja templating for creating network device configurations
  • A holistic approach to architecting network automation services
  • The role of application programming interfaces (APIs) in network automation
  • Source control with Git to manage code changes during the automation process
  • Cloud-native technologies like Docker and Kubernetes
  • How to automate network devices and services using Ansible, Nornir, and Terraform
  • Tools and technologies for developing and continuously integrating network automation

developers.developer-name: Matt Oswalt, Christian Adell, Scott S. Lowe, Jason Edelman

linuxcookbook.description_text:
This handy cookbook teaches new-to-intermediate Linux users the essential skills necessary to manage a Linux system, using both graphical and command-line tools. Whether you run Linux in embedded, desktop, server, or cloud or virtual environments, the fundamental skills are the same. This book aims to get you up and running quickly, with copy-paste examples.

Carla Schroder provides recipes that cover specific problems, with discussions that explain how each recipe works, as well as references for additional study.

You'll learn how to:

  • Use systemd, the new comprehensive service manager
  • Build simple or complex firewalls with firewalld
  • Set up secure network connections for Linux systems and mobile devices
  • Rescue nonbooting systems
  • Reset lost passwords on Linux and Windows
  • Use dnsmasq to simplify managing your LAN name services
  • Manage users and groups and control access to files
  • Probe your computer hardware and monitor hardware health
  • Manage the GRUB bootloader and multiboot Linux and Windows
  • Keep accurate time across your network with the newest tools
  • Build an internet router/firewall on Raspberry Pi
  • Manage filesystems and partitioning

codeforamerica.description_text:
Donations to support Code for America are made to  PayPal Giving Fund, subject to its policies.

Code for America is a national nonprofit that believes government can work for the people, by the people, in the 21st century. We organize a network of people who build technology to further local governments' priorities of creating healthy, prosperous, and safe communities. Our goal: government services that are simple, effective, and easy to use, for everyone.

kubernetes_upandrunning.description_text:
In just five years, Kubernetes has radically changed the way developers and ops personnel build, deploy, and maintain applications in the cloud. With this book's updated third edition, you'll learn how this popular container orchestrator can help your company achieve new levels of velocity, agility, reliability, and efficiency--whether you're new to distributed systems or have been deploying cloud native apps for some time.

Brendan Burns, Joe Beda, Kelsey Hightower, and Lachlan Evenson--who have worked on Kubernetes at Google and beyond--explain how this system fits into the life cycle of a distributed application. Software developers, engineers, and architects will learn ways to use tools and APIs to automate scalable distributed systems for online services, machine learning applications, or even a cluster of Raspberry Pi computers.

This guide shows you how to:

  • Create a simple cluster to learn how Kubernetes works
  • Dive into the details of deploying an application using Kubernetes
  • Learn specialized objects in Kubernetes, such as DaemonSets, jobs, ConfigMaps, and secrets
  • Explore deployments that tie together the lifecycle of a complete application
  • Get practical examples of how to develop and deploy real-world applications in Kubernetes

developers.developer-name: Brendan Burns, Joe Beda, Kelsey Hightower, Lachlan Evenson

learninggit.description_text:
This book teaches Git in a simple, visual, and tangible manner so that you can build a solid mental model of how Git version control works. Through the use of color, storytelling, and hands-on exercises, you will learn to use this tool with confidence.

The information is introduced incrementally so that you don't get bogged down with unknown terms or concepts. Learning Git is ideal for anyone who needs to use Git for personal or professional projects: coding bootcamp students, junior developers, data professionals, and technical writers, to name just a few!

This book covers how to:

  • Download Git and initialize a local repository
  • Add files to the staging area and make commits
  • Create, switch, and delete branches
  • Merge and rebase branches
  • Work with remote repositories including cloning, pushing, pulling, and fetching
  • Use pull requests to collaborate with others

efficientlinuxatthecommandline.human_name: Efficient Linux at the Command Line
efficientlinuxatthecommandline.description_text:
Take your Linux skills to the next level! Whether you're a system administrator, software developer, site reliability engineer, or enthusiastic hobbyist, this practical, hands-on book will help you work faster, smarter, and more efficiently. You'll learn how to create and run complex commands that solve real business problems, process and retrieve information, and automate manual tasks.

You'll also truly understand what happens behind the shell prompt, so no matter which commands you run, you can be more successful in everyday Linux use and more competitive on the job market.

As you build intermediate to advanced command-line skills, you'll learn how to:

  • Choose or construct commands that get your work done quickly
  • Run commands efficiently and navigate the Linux filesystem with ease
  • Build powerful, complex commands out of simpler ones
  • Transform text files and query them like databases to achieve business goals
  • Control Linux point-and-click features from the command line

versioncontrolwithgit.description_text:
Track, branch, merge, and manage code revisions with Git, the free and open source distributed version control system. Through a series of step-by-step tutorials, this practical guide quickly takes you from Git fundamentals to advanced techniques, and provides friendly yet rigorous advice for navigating Git's many functions. You'll learn how to work with everything from small to very large projects with speed and efficiency.

In this third edition, authors Prem Kumar Ponuthorai and Jon Loeliger break down Git concepts using a modular approach. You'll start with the basics and fundamental philosophy of Git, followed by intermediate commands to help you efficiently supplement your daily development workflow. Finally, you'll learn advanced Git commands and concepts to understand how Git works under the hood.

  • Learn how to use Git for real-world development scenarios
  • Gain insight into Git's common use cases, initial tasks, and basic functions
  • Use the system for distributed version control
  • Learn how to manage merges, conflicts, patches, and diffs
  • Apply advanced techniques such as rebasing, hooks, and ways to handle submodules

developers.developer-name: Prem Kumar Ponuthorai and Jon Loeliger

linuxobservabilitywithbpf.description_text:
Build your expertise in the BPF virtual machine in the Linux kernel with this practical guide for systems engineers. You‚Äôll not only dive into the BPF program lifecycle but also learn to write applications that observe and modify the kernel‚Äôs behavior; inject code to monitor, trace, and securely observe events in the kernel; and more.

Authors David Calavera and Lorenzo Fontana help you harness the power of BPF to make any computing system more observable. Familiarize yourself with the essential concepts you‚Äôll use on a day-to-day basis and augment your knowledge about performance optimization, networking, and security. Then see how it all comes together with code examples in C, Go, and Python.

  • Write applications that use BPF to observe and modify the Linux kernel‚Äôs behavior on demand
  • Inject code to monitor, trace, and observe events in the kernel in a secure way‚Äîno need to recompile the kernel or reboot the system
  • Explore code examples in C, Go, and Python
  • Gain a more thorough understanding of the BPF program lifecycle

practicallinuxsystemadministration.description_text:
This essential guide covers all aspects of Linux system administration, from user maintenance, backups, filesystem housekeeping, storage management, and network setup to hardware and software troubleshooting and some application management. It's both a practical daily reference manual for sysadmins and IT pros and a handy study guide for those taking Linux certification exams.

You'll turn to it frequently, not only because of the sheer volume of valuable information it provides but because of the real-world examples within and the clear, useful way the information is presented. With this book at your side, you'll be able to:

  • Install Linux and perform initial setup duties, such as connecting to a network
  • Navigate the Linux filesystem via the command line
  • Install software from repositories and source and satisfy dependencies
  • Set permissions on files and directories
  • Create, modify, and remove user accounts
  • Set up networking
  • Format and mount filesystems
  • Perform basic troubleshooting on hardware and software
  • Create and manage logical volumes
  • Work with SELinux
  • Manage a firewall and iptables
  • Shut down, reboot, and recover a system
  • Perform backups and restores

learningkalilinux.description_text:
With hundreds of tools preinstalled, the Kali Linux distribution makes it easier for security professionals to get started with security testing quickly. But with more than 600 tools in its arsenal, Kali Linux can also be overwhelming. The new edition of this practical book covers updates to the tools, including enhanced coverage of forensics and reverse engineering.

Author Ric Messier also goes beyond strict security testing by adding coverage on performing forensic analysis, including disk and memory forensics, as well as some basic malware analysis.

  • Explore the breadth of tools available on Kali Linux
  • Understand the value of security testing and examine the testing types available
  • Learn the basics of penetration testing through the entire attack lifecycle
  • Install Kali Linux on multiple systems, both physical and virtual
  • Discover how to use different security-focused tools
  • Structure a security test around Kali Linux tools
  • Extend Kali tools to create advanced attack techniques
  • Use Kali Linux to generate reports once testing is complete

linuxsystemprogramming.description_text:
Write software that draws directly on services offered by the Linux kernel and core system libraries. With this comprehensive book, Linux kernel contributor Robert Love provides you with a tutorial on Linux system programming, a reference manual on Linux system calls, and an insider‚Äôs guide to writing smarter, faster code.

Love clearly distinguishes between POSIX standard functions and special services offered only by Linux. With a new chapter on multithreading, this updated and expanded edition provides an in-depth look at Linux from both a theoretical and applied perspective over a wide range of programming topics, including:

  • A Linux kernel, C library, and C compiler overview
  • Basic I/O operations, such as reading from and writing to files
  • Advanced I/O interfaces, memory mappings, and optimization techniques
  • The family of system calls for basic process management
  • Advanced process management, including real-time processes
  • Thread concepts, multithreaded programming, and Pthreads
  • File and directory management
  • Interfaces for allocating memory and optimizing memory access
  • Basic and advanced signal interfaces, and their role on the system
  • Clock management, including POSIX clocks and high-resolution timers

understandingthelinuxkernel.description_text:
In order to thoroughly understand what makes Linux tick and why it works so well on a wide variety of systems, you need to delve deep into the heart of the kernel. The kernel handles all interactions between the CPU and the external world, and determines which programs will share processor time, in what order. It manages limited memory so well that hundreds of processes can share the system efficiently, and expertly organizes data transfers so that the CPU isn't kept waiting any longer than necessary for the relatively slow disks.

The third edition of Understanding the Linux Kernel takes you on a guided tour of the most significant data structures, algorithms, and programming tricks used in the kernel. Probing beyond superficial features, the authors offer valuable insights to people who want to know how things really work inside their machine. Important Intel-specific features are discussed. Relevant segments of code are dissected line by line. But the book covers more than just the functioning of the code; it explains the theoretical underpinnings of why Linux does things the way it does.

This edition of the book covers Version 2.6, which has seen significant changes to nearly every kernel subsystem, particularly in the areas of memory management and block devices. The book focuses on the following topics:

  • Memory management, including file buffering, process swapping, and Direct memory Access (DMA)
  • The Virtual Filesystem layer and the Second and Third Extended Filesystems
  • Process creation and scheduling
  • Signals, interrupts, and the essential interfaces to device drivers
  • Timing
  • Synchronization within the kernel
  • Interprocess Communication (IPC)
  • Program execution

Understanding the Linux Kernel will acquaint you with all the inner workings of Linux, but it's more than just an academic exercise. You'll learn what conditions bring out Linux's best performance, and you'll see how it meets the challenge of providing good system response during process scheduling, file access, and memory management in a wide variety of environments. This book will help you make the most of your Linux system.

linuxpocketguide.description_text:
If you use Linux in your day-to-day work, then Linux Pocket Guide is the perfect on-the-job reference. This thoroughly updated 20th anniversary edition explains more than 200 Linux commands, including new commands for file handling, package management, version control, file format conversions, and more.

In this concise guide, author Daniel Barrett provides the most useful Linux commands grouped by functionality. Whether you're a novice or an experienced user, this practical book is an ideal reference for the most important Linux commands.

You'll learn:

  • Essential concepts—commands, shells, users, and the filesystem
  • File commands-creating, organizing, manipulating, and processing files of all kinds
  • Sysadmin basics-superusers, processes, user management, and software installation
  • Filesystem maintenance-disks, RAID, logical volumes, backups, and more
  • Networking commands-working with hosts, network connections, email, and the web
  • Getting stuff done-everything from math to version control to graphics and audio

gitforteams.description_text:
You can do more with Git than just build software. This practical guide delivers a unique people-first approach to version control that also explains how using Git as a focal point can help your team work better together. You‚Äôll learn how to plan and pursue a Git workflow that not only ensures that you accomplish project goals, but also fits the immediate needs and future growth of your team.

The first part of the book on structuring workflow is useful for project managers, technical team leads, and CTOs. The second part provides hands-on exercises to help developers gain a better understanding of Git commands.

  • Explore the dynamics of team building
  • Walk through the process of creating and deploying software with Git
  • Structure workflow to influence the way your team collaborates
  • Learn a useful process for conducting code reviews
  • Set up a shared repository and identify specific team members as contributors, consumers, or maintainers
  • Know the why behind the Git commands your teammates use
  • Use branching strategies to separate different approaches to your project
  • Examine popular collaboration platforms: GitHub, Bitbucket, and GitLab

gitpocketguide.description_text:
This pocket guide is the perfect on-the-job companion to Git, the distributed version control system. It provides a compact, readable introduction to Git for new users, as well as a reference to common commands and procedures for those of you with Git experience.
